pseudocode.description: Corrected Lattice Construction & Lattice WER Algorithms

data_structures.LatticeBin: Set of word alternatives at position j {word1, word2, ...}
data_structures.Lattice: Array[0..len(reference)] → LatticeBin (words allowed at each position)
data_structures.VoteTable: votes[ref_pos][word] → count (number of models voting for word at ref_pos)

algorithm_build_lattice: FUNCTION BUILD_LATTICE(reference R, hypotheses H[1..m], threshold K):
votes ← [ {} for j in 0..len(R)-1 ]  // votes[pos][word] = count
// Collect votes from each model hypothesis
FOR each model m IN H[1..m]:
alignment ← ALIGN_WORDS(R, H[m])  // word-level alignment (e.g., Levenshtein)
FOR each (operation, ref_pos, hyp_word) IN alignment:
// Skip: deletion means word not in this model, no vote added
// Build lattice: add words with sufficient model agreement
IF count >= K * m:  // K is fraction threshold (e.g., 0.6)
// Note: Insertions typically handled as separate lattice positions
// or integrated into latticebin with special markers.
algorithm_lattice_edit_distance: dp ← [ [∞ for j in 0..n] for i in 0..m ]
dp[i][0] ← i  // i deletions from hyp
dp[0][j] ← j  // j insertions to match lattice

key_clarifications.voting_threshold: K = trust_threshold (e.g., 0.6), minimum fraction of models that must agree
key_clarifications.lattice_structure: Each lattice[j] is a SET of acceptable words at reference position j (reference word always included)
key_clarifications.alignment: Word-level alignment between reference and each hypothesis using edit distance
key_clarifications.lattice_wer_benefit: By allowing alternative words voted by multiple models, lattice WER typically lower than standard WER
